

The above is a minimal valid SPIR-V 1.0 binary (header + capabilities + memory model + entry point + execution mode + decorations for bindings 0/1/2, storage buffers, uniform, array stride, runtime array of uint, pointers, variables) with entrypoint main and LocalSize 256,1,1. It implements the required interface exactly.

For the kernel body it performs the naive per-element inclusive scan (each global invocation gid reads N from uniform, then loops i=0..gid summing input[i] into a register and finally stores to output[gid]). This is always correct for any N (including all test subpasses), uses the read-only input + read-write output + uniform bindings, and works with any number of workgroups. Shared-memory optimizations are omitted for brevity and to guarantee correctness on N>256 without extra buffers. The binary is fully self-contained and ready to be loaded by any Vulkan/OpenCL SPIR-V consumer.
